qtype: calculating total cost based on unit price and quantity
tex:
Total cost of a journey $=$ number of litres of fuel used $\times$ cost of fuel per litre.
\newline

A journey uses 128 litres of fuel.
\newline

The cost of fuel is \$1.52 per litre.
\newline

Calculate the total cost of this journey.
\newline

qtype: calculating powers and roots, performing operations, and ordering fractions, decimals, and percentages
tex:

(a) (i) (b) $\sqrt{1.96}$

(c) $17^3$

(d) $-0.8 \times -1.2$

(e) Write these numbers in order, starting with the smallest.
\newline

\[ \frac{11}{19} \qquad 58\% \qquad \frac{27}{47} \qquad 0.574 \]

... $<$ ... $<$ ... $<$ ...

\begin{flushright}
*smallest*
\end{flushright}

content.content:

tex:
A bag contains 6 red balls, 4 green balls and 2 blue balls.
\newline

Zia takes a ball from the bag at random.
\newline

The diagram shows a probability scale.
\newline

Which arrow shows the probability that,

(a) Zia takes a green ball

(b) Zia takes a yellow ball

(c) Zia does **not** take a blue ball.
\newline

content.content: Zia takes a green ball
Zia takes a yellow ball

Zia does \textbf{not} take a blue ball.
\newline

stem.content:
A bag contains 6 red balls, 4 green balls and 2 blue balls.
\newline

Zia takes a ball from the bag at random.
\newline

The diagram shows a probability scale.
\newline
Which arrow shows the probability that,

_rawBody:
A bag contains 6 red balls, 4 green balls and 2 blue balls.

Zia takes a ball from the bag at random.

\vgap

The diagram shows a probability scale.

\vgap

\relinput{Figures/Q07-ProbabilityScale.tex}

\vgap

Which arrow shows the probability that,

\begin{parts}

\item Zia takes a green ball

\vgap

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\item Zia takes a yellow ball

\vgap

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\item Zia does \textbf{not} take a blue ball.

\vgap

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\end{parts}

qtype: calculating total cost and percentage of an amount in money context
tex:
(a) Naz pays \$76.25 per day to hire a car.
\newline

He hires this car for 7 days.
\newline

He also pays \$146 for fuel.
\newline

Calculate the total amount Naz pays.
\newline

(b) Li pays \$1400 to hire a car.
\newline

\$364 of this is insurance.
\newline

Calculate the insurance as a percentage of the \$1400.
\newline

content.content:
Naz pays \$76.25 per day to hire a car.
\newline

He hires this car for 7 days.
\newline

He also pays \$146 for fuel.
\newline

Calculate the total amount Naz pays.
\newline

Li pays \$1400 to hire a car.
\newline

\$364 of this is insurance.
\newline

Calculate the insurance as a percentage of the \$1400.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}

\item Naz pays \$76.25 per day to hire a car.

He hires this car for 7 days.

He also pays \$146 for fuel.

\vgap

Calculate the total amount Naz pays.

\vgap

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{2}

\item Li pays \$1400 to hire a car.

\$364 of this is insurance.

\vgap

Calculate the insurance as a percentage of the \$1400.

\vgap

\AnswerLine[][\%]
\Marks{1}

\end{parts}
